sentences.text: This chapter describes the RV64I base integer instruction set, which builds upon the RV32I variant described in .
sentences.section: Preamble > RV64I Base Integer Instruction Set, Version 2.1

sentences.text: This chapter presents only the differences with RV32I, so should be read in conjunction with the earlier chapter.
sentences.section: Preamble > RV64I Base Integer Instruction Set, Version 2.1

sentences.text: RV64I widens the integer registers and supported user address space to 64 bits (XLEN=64 in ).
sentences.section: Preamble > RV64I Base Integer Instruction Set, Version 2.1 > Register State

sentences.text: Most integer computational instructions operate on XLEN-bit values.
sentences.section: Preamble > RV64I Base Integer Instruction Set, Version 2.1 > Integer Computational Instructions

sentences.text: Additional instruction variants are provided to manipulate 32-bit values in RV64I, indicated by a 'W' suffix to the opcode.
sentences.section: Preamble > RV64I Base Integer Instruction Set, Version 2.1 > Integer Computational Instructions

sentences.text: These "*W" instructions ignore the upper 32 bits of their inputs and always produce 32-bit signed values, sign-extending them to 64 bits, i.e. bits XLEN-1 through 31 are equal.
sentences.section: Preamble > RV64I Base Integer Instruction Set, Version 2.1 > Integer Computational Instructions

sentences.text: ADDIW is an RV64I instruction that adds the sign-extended 12-bit immediate to register rs1 and produces the proper sign extension of a 32-bit result in rd.
sentences.section: Preamble > RV64I Base Integer Instruction Set, Version 2.1 > Integer Computational Instructions > Integer Register-Immediate Instructions

sentences.text: Overflows are ignored and the result is the low 32 bits of the result sign-extended to 64 bits.
sentences.section: Preamble > RV64I Base Integer Instruction Set, Version 2.1 > Integer Computational Instructions > Integer Register-Immediate Instructions

sentences.text: Note, ADDIW rd, rs1, 0 writes the sign extension of the lower 32 bits of register rs1 into register rd (assembler pseudoinstruction SEXT.W).
sentences.section: Preamble > RV64I Base Integer Instruction Set, Version 2.1 > Integer Computational Instructions > Integer Register-Immediate Instructions

sentences.text: Shifts by a constant are encoded as a specialization of the I-type format using the same instruction opcode as RV32I.
sentences.section: Preamble > RV64I Base Integer Instruction Set, Version 2.1 > Integer Computational Instructions > Integer Register-Immediate Instructions

sentences.text: The operand to be shifted is in rs1, and the shift amount is encoded in the lower 6 bits of the I-immediate field for RV64I.
sentences.section: Preamble > RV64I Base Integer Instruction Set, Version 2.1 > Integer Computational Instructions > Integer Register-Immediate Instructions

sentences.text: The right-shift type is encoded in bit 30. SLLI is a logical left shift (zeros are shifted into the lower bits); SRLI is a logical right shift (zeros are shifted into the upper bits); and SRAI is an arithmetic right shift (the original sign bit is copied into the vacated upper bits).
sentences.section: Preamble > RV64I Base Integer Instruction Set, Version 2.1 > Integer Computational Instructions > Integer Register-Immediate Instructions

sentences.text: SLLIW, SRLIW, and SRAIW are RV64I-only instructions that are analogously defined but operate on 32-bit values and sign-extend their 32-bit results to 64 bits. SLLIW, SRLIW, and SRAIW encodings with imm[5] {ne} 0 are reserved.
sentences.section: Preamble > RV64I Base Integer Instruction Set, Version 2.1 > Integer Computational Instructions > Integer Register-Immediate Instructions

sentences.text: LUI (load upper immediate) uses the same opcode as RV32I. LUI places the 32-bit U-immediate into register rd, filling in the lowest 12 bits with zeros.
sentences.section: Preamble > RV64I Base Integer Instruction Set, Version 2.1 > Integer Computational Instructions > Integer Register-Immediate Instructions

sentences.text: The 32-bit result is sign-extended to 64 bits.
sentences.section: Preamble > RV64I Base Integer Instruction Set, Version 2.1 > Integer Computational Instructions > Integer Register-Immediate Instructions

sentences.text: AUIPC (add upper immediate to `pc`) uses the same opcode as RV32I. AUIPC is used to build `pc`-relative addresses and uses the U-type format. AUIPC forms a 32-bit offset from the U-immediate, filling in the lowest 12 bits with zeros, sign-extends the result to 64 bits, adds it to the address of the AUIPC instruction, then places the result in register rd.
sentences.section: Preamble > RV64I Base Integer Instruction Set, Version 2.1 > Integer Computational Instructions > Integer Register-Immediate Instructions

sentences.text: ADDW and SUBW are RV64I-only instructions that are defined analogously to ADD and SUB but operate on 32-bit values and produce signed 32-bit results.
sentences.section: Preamble > RV64I Base Integer Instruction Set, Version 2.1 > Integer Computational Instructions > Integer Register-Register Operations

sentences.text: Overflows are ignored, and the low 32-bits of the result is sign-extended to 64-bits and written to the destination register.
sentences.section: Preamble > RV64I Base Integer Instruction Set, Version 2.1 > Integer Computational Instructions > Integer Register-Register Operations

sentences.text: SLL, SRL, and SRA perform logical left, logical right, and arithmetic right shifts on the value in register rs1 by the shift amount held in register rs2.
sentences.section: Preamble > RV64I Base Integer Instruction Set, Version 2.1 > Integer Computational Instructions > Integer Register-Register Operations

sentences.text: In RV64I, only the low 6 bits of rs2 are considered for the shift amount.
sentences.section: Preamble > RV64I Base Integer Instruction Set, Version 2.1 > Integer Computational Instructions > Integer Register-Register Operations

sentences.text: SLLW, SRLW, and SRAW are RV64I-only instructions that are analogously defined but operate on 32-bit values and sign-extend their 32-bit results to 64 bits.
sentences.section: Preamble > RV64I Base Integer Instruction Set, Version 2.1 > Integer Computational Instructions > Integer Register-Register Operations

sentences.text: The shift amount is given by rs2[4:0].
sentences.section: Preamble > RV64I Base Integer Instruction Set, Version 2.1 > Integer Computational Instructions > Integer Register-Register Operations

sentences.text: RV64I extends the address space to 64 bits.
sentences.section: Preamble > RV64I Base Integer Instruction Set, Version 2.1 > Load and Store Instructions

sentences.text: The execution environment will define what portions of the address space are legal to access.
sentences.section: Preamble > RV64I Base Integer Instruction Set, Version 2.1 > Load and Store Instructions

sentences.text: The LD instruction loads a 64-bit value from memory into register rd for RV64I.
sentences.section: Preamble > RV64I Base Integer Instruction Set, Version 2.1 > Load and Store Instructions

sentences.text: The LW instruction loads a 32-bit value from memory and sign-extends this to 64 bits before storing it in register rd for RV64I.
sentences.section: Preamble > RV64I Base Integer Instruction Set, Version 2.1 > Load and Store Instructions

sentences.text: The LWU instruction, on the other hand, zero-extends the 32-bit value from memory for RV64I. LH and LHU are defined analogously for 16-bit values, as are LB and LBU for 8-bit values.
sentences.section: Preamble > RV64I Base Integer Instruction Set, Version 2.1 > Load and Store Instructions

sentences.text: The SD, SW, SH, and SB instructions store 64-bit, 32-bit, 16-bit, and 8-bit values from the low bits of register rs2 to memory respectively.
sentences.section: Preamble > RV64I Base Integer Instruction Set, Version 2.1 > Load and Store Instructions

sentences.text: All instructions that are microarchitectural HINTs in RV32I (see ) are also HINTs in RV64I.
sentences.section: Preamble > RV64I Base Integer Instruction Set, Version 2.1 > HINT Instructions

sentences.text: The additional computational instructions in RV64I expand both the standard and custom HINT encoding spaces.
sentences.section: Preamble > RV64I Base Integer Instruction Set, Version 2.1 > HINT Instructions

sentences.text: lists all RV64I HINT code points. 91% of the HINT space is reserved for standard HINTs.
sentences.section: Preamble > RV64I Base Integer Instruction Set, Version 2.1 > HINT Instructions

sentences.text: The remainder of the HINT space is designated for custom HINTs; no standard HINTs will ever be defined in this subspace.
sentences.section: Preamble > RV64I Base Integer Instruction Set, Version 2.1 > HINT Instructions

sentences.text: .RV64I HINT instructions. [float="center",align="center",cols="<,<,^,<", options="header", grid="all"] | |Instruction |Constraints |Code Points |Purpose |LUI |rd=`x0` |2^20^ .9+.^|Designated for future standard use
sentences.section: Preamble > RV64I Base Integer Instruction Set, Version 2.1 > HINT Instructions

sentences.text: |ADDI |rd=`x0`, and either rs1{ne}`x0` or imm{ne}0 |2^17^−1
sentences.section: Preamble > RV64I Base Integer Instruction Set, Version 2.1 > HINT Instructions
Preamble > RV64I Base Integer Instruction Set, Version 2.1 > HINT Instructions

sentences.text: |ADD |rd=`x0`, rs1=`x0`, rs2{ne}x2-x5 | 28
sentences.section: Preamble > RV64I Base Integer Instruction Set, Version 2.1 > HINT Instructions

sentences.text: |ADD |rd=`x0`, rs1=`x0`, rs2=x2-x5| 4 | (rs2=x2) NTL.P1 + (rs2=x3) NTL.PALL + (rs2=x4) NTL.S1 + (rs2=x5) NTL.ALL
sentences.section: Preamble > RV64I Base Integer Instruction Set, Version 2.1 > HINT Instructions

sentences.text: |SLLI |rd=`x0`, rs1=`x0`, shamt=31 |1|Semihosting entry marker
sentences.section: Preamble > RV64I Base Integer Instruction Set, Version 2.1 > HINT Instructions

sentences.text: |SRAI |rd=`x0`, rs1=`x0`, shamt=7 |1|Semihosting exit marker
sentences.section: Preamble > RV64I Base Integer Instruction Set, Version 2.1 > HINT Instructions

sentences.text: |SUB |rd=`x0` |2^10^ .16+.^| Designated for future standard use
sentences.section: Preamble > RV64I Base Integer Instruction Set, Version 2.1 > HINT Instructions

sentences.text: |FENCE |rd=`x0`, rs1{ne}``x0``, fm=0, and either pred=0 or succ=0| 2^10^−63
sentences.section: Preamble > RV64I Base Integer Instruction Set, Version 2.1 > HINT Instructions

sentences.text: |FENCE |rd{ne}``x0``, rs1=`x0`, fm=0, and either pred=0 or succ=0| 2^10^−63
sentences.section: Preamble > RV64I Base Integer Instruction Set, Version 2.1 > HINT Instructions

sentences.text: |FENCE |rd=rs1=`x0`, fm=0, pred=0, succ{ne}0 |15
sentences.section: Preamble > RV64I Base Integer Instruction Set, Version 2.1 > HINT Instructions

sentences.text: |FENCE |rd=rs1=`x0`, fm=0, pred{ne}W, succ=0 |15
sentences.section: Preamble > RV64I Base Integer Instruction Set, Version 2.1 > HINT Instructions

sentences.text: |FENCE |rd=rs1=`x0`, fm=0, pred=W, succ=0 |1 |PAUSE
sentences.section: Preamble > RV64I Base Integer Instruction Set, Version 2.1 > HINT Instructions

sentences.text: |SLTI |rd=`x0` |2^17^ .10+.^|Designated for custom use
sentences.section: Preamble > RV64I Base Integer Instruction Set, Version 2.1 > HINT Instructions

sentences.text: |SLLI |rd=`x0`, and either rs1{ne}``x0`` or shamt{ne}31 |2^11^−1
sentences.section: Preamble > RV64I Base Integer Instruction Set, Version 2.1 > HINT Instructions

sentences.text: |SRAI |rd=`x0`, and either rs1{ne}``x0`` or shamt{ne}7 |2^11^−1
sentences.section: Preamble > RV64I Base Integer Instruction Set, Version 2.1 > HINT Instructions

sentences.text: NOTE: `slli x0, x0, 0x1f` and `srai x0, x0, 7` were previously designated as custom HINTs, but they have been appropriated for use in semihosting calls, as described in .
sentences.section: Preamble > RV64I Base Integer Instruction Set, Version 2.1 > HINT Instructions

sentences.text: To reflect their usage in practice, the base ISA spec has been changed to designate them as standard HINTs.
sentences.section: Preamble > RV64I Base Integer Instruction Set, Version 2.1 > HINT Instructions
